name: London Gatwick Airport (LGW)
content: Good rail connections to the city. During my first visit to London, when I had just left the airport and boarded a train to the city, a man boarded the train about halfway through my journey armed with a bicycle and a Tesco shopping bag. From the Tesco bag he produced a package of biscuits, a bottle of red wine, and a bottle of white wine. I disembarked the train twenty minutes later, and for those twenty minutes, the man would alternate between eating a biscuit, taking a swig of white wine, gulping down some red wine (with a formidable amount of lip smacking), and then returning for another biscuit. Thus went my first ever experience of observing a British person from up close.
